opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My cheek is covered in these burning blisters that popped up overnight, and they are spreading right up to my eye. It hurts to even blink.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: They came up overnight and they're creeping up toward my eye — I'm really scared it's going to get into my eye and damage my sight.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 days ago I felt a tingling, burning patch on my right upper cheek just under my eye, and then about 12 hours ago a cluster of little blisters erupted there
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 48 hours since the tingling, with the actual blisters coming up in the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a tight cluster of small fluid-filled blisters on a red base; some have already popped and left little raw spots with a golden crust; it burns and there's a mild throbbing
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the burning is the worst part; the pain itself is only a mild throb, but I'm frightened because it's so close to my eye
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my right cheek just below my eye, close to my lower eyelid — it feels like it's inching toward my eye
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the tingling came first, then the blisters erupted overnight and have been there since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; a cool touch helps the burning for a moment
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: blinking and the fact that it's so near my eye makes me more anxious
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my eye itself doesn't hurt and my vision seems normal to me, my eye doesn't look red, I don't have a fever, no blisters on my forehead or the tip of my nose, and no numbness or deep nerve pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get the odd cold sore on my lip now and then, but never a big cluster like this on my cheek near my eye
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm coping with day-to-day things but I'm very anxious and I live on my own

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: occasional cold sores on my lip in the past

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been under a lot of stress lately and I was out at the beach in the sun a few days before this came up; I live alone

objective_data_offered.value: a roughly 2 cm cluster of small tense fluid-filled blisters on a red base on my right cheek just below the eye; a few have burst leaving shallow raw spots with a golden/honey crust starting
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described and shown by the patient over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: it's all in one patch on the right cheek near the lower eyelid — only on one side of my face, nothing on my forehead, eyelid rim or the tip of my nose
my eye doesn't look red to me and I can see normally out of it; there's no discharge and lights don't bother me
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: the patient's own impression only — NOT an eye examination; cornea, visual acuity and early dendritic keratitis cannot be assessed this way

objective_data_offered.value: alert and talking normally but visibly anxious and shielding the right side of the face
I haven't taken my temperature but I don't feel hot or feverish, just a bit sore and anxious
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken
